docs.title: CareConnect
docs.text: A verified, governance-first search engine for social services in Kingston, Ontario—covering food security, crisis intervention, and housing support.

docs.title: Manual Curation, Not Scraping
docs.text:
Large-scale scraping of municipal data produces noise, not value. CareConnect takes a different path: manual curation over automated extraction.

We maintain a hand-verified dataset of the 196 highest-impact services available to Kingston residents. Every entry is:

  • Verified — No broken links or disconnected phone numbers.
  • Accessible — Clear eligibility requirements.
  • Identity-Aware — Evidence-backed safety tags for vulnerable populations.

docs.title: Current Version
docs.text:
v22.0 - Non-Duplicate Value Decision Plan, Phase 0 (2026-03-29)

Current operating state: Gate 0 remains NO-GO until legal/API review (C1) and partner-ops execution evidence (D4) are closed. The retention/privacy control (C2) is complete. See docs/planning/roadmap.md for the canonical project state.

docs.title: Performance & Resilience (v17.5)
docs.text:
• Performance Tracking: Real-time operation metrics with p50/p95/p99 latency tracking
  • Circuit Breaker Pattern: Prevents cascading failures when database is unavailable (fast-fail in <1ms)
  • Health Check API: Public and authenticated endpoints for system monitoring
  • Metrics Endpoint: Development-only API for operational visibility
  • Load Testing Infrastructure: k6 tests for baseline metrics and regression detection

docs.title: Partner Portal & Dashboard (v17.4)
docs.text:
• Organization Management: Create organizations and manage members with role-based access
  • Service Management: Partners can create, edit, publish, and delete their service listings
  • Analytics Dashboard: View search analytics and user feedback patterns
  • RBAC System: 4 role tiers (Owner, Admin, Editor, Viewer) with 19 granular permissions
  • Member Management: Invite members, change roles, and manage access

docs.title: Accessibility & Compliance (v17.3)
docs.text:
• WCAG 2.1 AA Compliant: High-contrast mode, skip-links, and keyboard navigation
  • Comprehensive Testing: Automated accessibility audits with Axe-core
  • Voice Input: Natural language voice search support

docs.title: Internationalization (v17.2)
docs.text:
• 7 Languages: Full support for English, Canadian French, Arabic, Simplified Chinese, Spanish, Punjabi, Portuguese
  • RTL Support: Full right-to-left layout for Arabic interface
  • Locale-Aware Search: Language-specific synonyms and search results

docs.title: Security & Authorization (v17.0)
docs.text:
• Row-Level Security: Database-enforced access controls per organization
  • RBAC System: Comprehensive permission matrix for authorization
  • Session Management: Secure authentication with Supabase Auth
  • Audit Trails: Detailed logging of user actions and changes

docs.title: Legal & Compliance Infrastructure
docs.text:
• Enforceable Protections: Robust Terms of Service and Privacy Policy (PIPEDA/PHIPA compliant).
  • Emergency Safeguards: Prominent disclaimers and immediate 911/988 access on crisis pages.
  • AI Transparency: Detailed disclaimers for browser-based AI features.
  • AODA Compliance: Dedicated Accessibility Policy and multi-year compliance plan.
  • Governance Audit: Public-facing Content Moderation Policy and Feedback Process.
  • Entity Preparedness: Documented research for Non-Profit incorporation and liability insurance.

docs.title: Search Intelligence
docs.text:
• Synonym Expansion: "Hungry" returns food banks; "rent" surfaces eviction prevention resources.
  • Open Now Filter: Real-time availability based on structured operating hours.
  • Privacy-First Analytics: Tracks unmet needs through zero-result patterns without logging queries.
  • Crisis Detection: Automatically boosts emergency services when high-risk language is detected.
  • Search Explainability: Public result cards and linked detail pages can show deduplicated match reasons for why a service ranked.
  • Stale-Data Governance: Records beyond the 180-day freshness window are hidden from search instead of lingering with only a soft score penalty.
  • Map Integration: External map previews stay off by default; users can explicitly open directions or load a map preview on service detail pages.

docs.title: Decentralized AI Assistant
docs.text:
• On-Device Smart Search: Uses a small local LLM (WebLLM + WebGPU) to rewrite/expand natural-language queries for better matching.
  • Deterministic Results: The UI renders verified service links from the local directory (no free-form “chatbot answers” shown to users).
  • Zero-Knowledge Architecture: Queries never leave the device.
  • Offline-Friendly: The service directory and embeddings can be cached for offline search.

docs.title: Librarian Model (v13.0)
docs.text:
• Server-Side Search API: Privacy-focused, rate-limited POST endpoint for enhanced security.
  • Zero-Logging: Search queries are strictly no-store and never logged to the database.
  • Dynamic Bundle: Falls back to lightweight server queries, saving ~300KB on initial load.

docs.title: Additional Capabilities
docs.text:
• 196 Verified Services — Hand-curated Kingston services across 12 categories.
  • Semantic and Fuzzy Search — Natural language queries ("I feel unsafe") and typo correction ("fod" → "food").
  • Privacy by Design — No cookies, no tracking, no search logging. All inference runs in-browser or anonymously.
  • Service Detail Pages — Rich metadata, contact information, and localized content for each listing.
  • Partner Claiming Workflow — Organizations can claim, verify, and maintain their own listings.
  • Progressive Web App — Installable, works offline.
  • WCAG 2.1 AA Compliant — High-contrast, skip-links, and keyboard navigation.
  • Community Governance — Residents can flag inaccurate data directly.
  • Performance Optimized — Loads instantly, even on slow connections.
  • Trust Signals — Visible freshness badges, provenance data, and explicit stale-record warnings for direct links beyond the governance freshness window.
  • Multi-Lingual Support — Full support for 7 languages: English, Canadian French, Arabic, Simplified Chinese, Spanish, Punjabi, and Portuguese.
  • Indigenous Health Services — Dedicated filters and culturally safe tags.
  • Land Acknowledgment — Respecting the traditional lands of Kingston (Katarokwi).
  • Provincial Crisis Lines — 16 Ontario-wide crisis services (988, ConnexOntario, Kids Help Phone, etc.).

docs.title: Tech Stack
docs.text: Layer Technology Framework Next.js 15 (App Router) Language TypeScript Styling Tailwind CSS v4 UI Components Radix UI AI / Embeddings @xenova/transformers Testing Vitest · Playwright Database Supabase (PostgreSQL + pgvector)

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Node.js 22+
  • npm 10+

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/jerdaw/careconnect.git # (1)
cd careconnect
npm install
npm run dev
  1. This clones the main repository to your local machine.

Open http://localhost:3000 to view the application.

docs.title: Scripts
docs.text:
Development & Testing: | Command | Description | | :------------------------ | :-------------------------------------- | | npm run dev | Start development server (Turbo) | | npm run build | Build for production | | npm run start | Start production server | | npm test | Run the default Vitest suite | | npm run test:db | Run real DB integration tests locally (Docker + psql) | | npm run test:watch | Vitest in watch mode | | npm run test:coverage | Generate coverage report | | npm run test:e2e | Run E2E tests (all browsers) | | npm run test:e2e:local | Run E2E tests (Chromium only) | | npm run test:a11y | Run accessibility audit (Axe-core) | | npm run type-check | TypeScript compiler check | | npm run lint | ESLint code quality check | | npm run lint:fix | ESLint with auto-fix | | npm run format | Format code with Prettier | | npm run format:check | Check code formatting | | npm run ci:check | Run CI validation checks (DB lane auto-skips locally if prerequisites are missing) | | npm run check:root | Check project root hygiene |

Load Testing (v17.5): | Command | Description | | :------------------------ | :-------------------------------------- | | npm run test:load | Run search API load test (realistic) | | npm run test:load:smoke | Run smoke test (basic connectivity) | | npm run test:load:sustained | Run sustained load test (30 minutes) | | npm run test:load:spike | Run spike test (sudden traffic spike) |

Data Validation & Audits: | Command | Description | | :------------------------ | :----------------------------------------------- | | npm run validate-data | Validate data schema (Zod) | | npm run db:validate | Alias for validate-data | | npm run db:verify | Verify database integrity (row count, RLS) | | npm run health-check | Validate all service URLs | | npm run phone-validate | Validate phone numbers (Twilio) | | npm run check-staleness | Check for stale/unverified data | | npm run audit:data | Comprehensive data completeness audit | | npm run audit:qa | Data quality and integrity audit | | npm run audit:coords | Export services with missing coordinates | | npm run audit:hours | Export services with missing operating hours | | npm run audit:access-scripts | Audit access_script quality | | npm run audit:l3 | Export L3 verification candidates | | npm run bilingual-check | Check bilingual content coverage | | npm run i18n-audit | Audit i18n translation key coverage | | npm run analyze | Analyze production bundle size |

Data Enrichment & Translation: | Command | Description | | :------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------- | | npm run export:access-script-fr | Export access_script fields for French | | npm run translate:prompt | Generate AI translation prompts | | npm run translate:parse | Parse AI response into structured JSON | | npm run translate:validate | Validate translation batch | | npm run backfill:hours-text | Backfill hours_text from structured hours | | npm run geocode | Geocode addresses (requires OPENCAGE_API_KEY) |

See French Translation Workflow for detailed translation process.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Copy .env.example to .env.local. Core search functionality works without API keys; database features require Supabase credentials.

For Librarian Model (Server-Side Search):

NEXT_PUBLIC_SEARCH_MODE=server

(Defaults to local if unset).

docs.title: Partner Platform (Supabase)
docs.text:
To enable the Partner Portal, authentication, and analytics:

  1. Create a project at database.new.
  2. Add your credentials to .env.local:
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://your-project.supabase.co
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=your-publishable-key
SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY=your-secret-key
  1. Apply the schema via Supabase CLI migrations:
npx supabase link --project-ref your-project-ref
npx supabase db push
  1. Migrate local data:
npx tsx scripts/migrate-data.ts

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: This project is community-led. Safety and accuracy take precedence over volume.

docs.title: Adding a Service
docs.text:
Proposed services must meet these criteria:

  • Serves the Kingston, Ontario area.
  • Has a verifiable phone number or physical address.
  • Free or subsidized.

MIT License

docs.title: Accessibility Guide
docs.text: This guide provides best practices for maintaining WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility compliance in the CareConnect platform.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Image Alt Text Guidelines
  • ARIA Best Practices
  • Keyboard Navigation Patterns
  • Color and Contrast
  • Form Accessibility

docs.title: When to Use Alt Text
docs.text: Every image must have an alt attribute, even if it's empty. The content of the alt attribute depends on the image's purpose:

docs.title: 1. Decorative Images
docs.text:
If an image is purely decorative and doesn't convey information:

<img src="/divider.png" alt="" aria-hidden="true" />

Examples:

  • Background patterns
  • Visual dividers
  • Decorative borders
  • Spacer graphics

docs.title: 2. Functional Images (Icons, Buttons)
docs.text:
If an image functions as a button or interactive element, describe its function, not its appearance:

// Good
<img src="/icon-search.svg" alt="Search" />
<img src="/icon-menu.svg" alt="Open menu" />
<img src="/icon-close.svg" alt="Close dialog" />

// Bad
<img src="/icon-search.svg" alt="magnifying glass icon" />

docs.title: 3. Informational Images
docs.text:
Describe the content and meaning of the image concisely:

// Good: Specific and concise
<img src="/service-photo.jpg" alt="Kingston Community Health Center building entrance" />

// Bad: Too vague
<img src="/service-photo.jpg" alt="building" />

// Bad: Too verbose
<img
  src="/service-photo.jpg"
  alt="This is a photo of the brick building where Kingston Community Health Center is located at the corner of Main Street and Princess Street with a blue door and wheelchair ramp"
/>

Alt text should:

  • Be concise (typically under 125 characters)
  • Not include "image of" or "picture of" (screen readers announce this automatically)
  • Convey the same information as the image
  • End with a period if it's a complete sentence

docs.title: 4. Complex Images (Charts, Graphs, Maps)
docs.text:
For complex images that require extended descriptions:

<figure>
  <img src="/service-distribution-map.png" alt="Map showing distribution of health services across Kingston" />
  <figcaption>
    <details>
      <summary>Detailed service distribution information</summary>
      <p>
        The map shows 15 health services clustered in downtown Kingston, 8 services in the east end near Princess
        Street, and 5 services in the west end near Division Street. Rural areas have 3 services distributed along
        Highway 2.
      </p>
    </details>
  </figcaption>
</figure>

docs.title: 5. Images in Links
docs.text:
When an image is the only content in a link, the alt text should describe the link destination, not just the image:

// Good: Describes where the link goes
<Link href="/service/123">
  <img src="/icon-health.svg" alt="View Kingston Community Health Center details" />
</Link>

// Bad: Only describes the image
<Link href="/service/123">
  <img src="/icon-health.svg" alt="health icon" />
</Link>

// Also acceptable if there's visible text
<Link href="/service/123">
  <img src="/icon-health.svg" alt="" aria-hidden="true" />
  <span>View Health Services</span>
</Link>

docs.title: 6. Logo Images
docs.text:
Logos should use the organization name:

// Good
<img src="/logo.svg" alt="CareConnect Logo" />
<img src="/partner-logo.svg" alt={`${partner.name} Logo`} />

// Bad
<img src="/logo.svg" alt="Logo" />

docs.title: 7. Dynamic Images
docs.text:
For user-generated or dynamic content:

// Service provider photos
<img
  src={service.imageUrl}
  alt={service.imageAlt || `${service.name} service location`}
/>

// User avatars
<img
  src={user.avatarUrl}
  alt={`${user.name}'s profile picture`}
/>

docs.title: When to Use ARIA
docs.text:
First rule of ARIA: Don't use ARIA. Use semantic HTML instead.

// Good: Semantic HTML
<button onClick={handleClick}>Submit</button>

// Bad: div with ARIA
<div role="button" onClick={handleClick} tabIndex={0}>Submit</div>

docs.title: 1. Form Fields with Errors
docs.text:
<div>
  <label htmlFor="email">Email Address</label>
  <input
    id="email"
    type="email"
    aria-describedby={error ? "email-error" : undefined}
    aria-invalid={!!error}
    aria-required
  />
  {error && (
    <p id="email-error" role="alert">
      {error}
    </p>
  )}
</div>

docs.title: 2. Live Regions
docs.text:
// Announce search results count
<div aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="true">
  {searchResults.length} services found
</div>

// Urgent announcements
<div aria-live="assertive" role="alert">
  Your session will expire in 5 minutes
</div>

docs.title: 3. Expandable Sections
docs.text:
<button
  aria-expanded={isExpanded}
  aria-controls="section-content"
  onClick={() => setIsExpanded(!isExpanded)}
>
  {isExpanded ? 'Hide' : 'Show'} Details
</button>
<div id="section-content" hidden={!isExpanded}>
  {/* Content */}
</div>

docs.title: 4. Modal Dialogs
docs.text:
Use the Radix Dialog component, which handles ARIA automatically:

import { Dialog, DialogContent, DialogTitle, DialogDescription } from "@/components/ui/dialog"
;<Dialog open={isOpen} onOpenChange={setIsOpen}>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogTitle>Confirm Action</DialogTitle>
    <DialogDescription>Are you sure you want to proceed?</DialogDescription>
    {/* Content */}
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

docs.title: Essential Keyboard Support
docs.text:
Every interactive element must be keyboard accessible:

Element Required Keys Button Enter, Space Link Enter Checkbox Space Radio Group Arrow keys, Space Select Dropdown Enter, Arrow keys, Esc Modal/Dialog Esc to close, focus trap Menu Arrow keys, Enter, Esc

docs.title: Focus Management
docs.text:
// After closing modal, return focus to trigger
const triggerRef = useRef<HTMLButtonElement>(null)

const openModal = () => {
  setIsOpen(true)
}

const closeModal = () => {
  setIsOpen(false)
  triggerRef.current?.focus()
}

return (
  <>
    <button ref={triggerRef} onClick={openModal}>
      Open Modal
    </button>
    <Modal isOpen={isOpen} onClose={closeModal} />
  </>
)

docs.title: Skip Links
docs.text:
Every page should have a skip link as the first focusable element:

;<a href="#main-content" className="sr-only focus:not-sr-only focus:fixed focus:top-4 focus:left-4 focus:z-50">
  Skip to main content
</a>

{
  /* ... header/nav ... */
}

;<main id="main-content" tabIndex={-1}>
  {/* Page content */}
</main>

docs.title: WCAG AA Requirements
docs.text:
• Normal text: 4.5:1 contrast ratio
  • Large text (18pt+ or 14pt+ bold): 3:1 contrast ratio
  • UI components and graphics: 3:1 contrast ratio

docs.title: Testing Tools
docs.text:
• Chrome DevTools (Lighthouse)
  • WebAIM Contrast Checker
  • Axe DevTools extension

docs.title: Tailwind Color Safety
docs.text:
When using Tailwind, follow these guidelines:

// Safe for body text on white background
className = "text-gray-900" // ✅ 21:1
className = "text-gray-800" // ✅ 15:1
className = "text-gray-700" // ✅ 10.5:1

// Use sparingly or for large text only
className = "text-gray-600" // ⚠️ 7:1 (borderline)
className = "text-gray-500" // ❌ 4.6:1 (fails for normal text)

docs.title: Don't Rely on Color Alone
docs.text:
// Bad: Only color indicates status
<span className="text-red-600">Error</span>
<span className="text-green-600">Success</span>

// Good: Icon + color + text
<span className="text-red-600">
  <AlertCircle className="inline h-4 w-4" /> Error
</span>
<span className="text-green-600">
  <CheckCircle className="inline h-4 w-4" /> Success
</span>

docs.title: Use the AccessibleFormField Component
docs.text:
Always use the AccessibleFormField wrapper for consistent accessibility:

import { AccessibleFormField } from "@/components/forms/AccessibleFormField"
;<AccessibleFormField
  label="Email Address"
  id="email"
  error={errors.email}
  hint="We'll never share your email"
  required
>
  <input type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@example.com" />
</AccessibleFormField>

This automatically provides:

  • <label> association via htmlFor
  • aria-invalid on errors
  • aria-describedby linking errors/hints
  • role="alert" on error messages
  • aria-required for required fields

docs.title: Grouped Fields
docs.text:
For radio buttons or checkboxes, use <fieldset> and <legend>:

<fieldset>
  <legend>Service Category</legend>
  <div role="group">
    <label>
      <input type="radio" name="category" value="health" />
      Health Services
    </label>
    <label>
      <input type="radio" name="category" value="housing" />
      Housing Services
    </label>
  </div>
</fieldset>

docs.title: Error Announcements
docs.text:
Errors should be announced to screen readers immediately:

{
  errors.length > 0 && (
    <div role="alert" aria-live="assertive">
      {errors.length} errors found. Please review the form.
    </div>
  )
}

docs.title: Testing Checklist
docs.text:
Before submitting code, verify:

  • [ ] All images have alt attributes
  • [ ] Color contrast meets 4.5:1 (use Axe DevTools)
  • [ ] All interactive elements are keyboard accessible
  • [ ] Focus indicators are visible
  • [ ] Forms have proper labels and error handling
  • [ ] Modals trap focus and close on Esc
  • [ ] No ESLint a11y violations (npm run lint)
  • [ ] Accessibility tests pass (npm run test:a11y)

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
• WCAG 2.1 Quick Reference
  • WebAIM Articles
  • Radix UI Accessibility
  • MDN ARIA Guide
  • Axe-core Rule Descriptions

docs.title: Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (2005)
docs.text: CareConnect is committed to providing a barrier-free service to people with disabilities. We strive to ensure our platform is accessible to all users, regardless of ability or technology.

docs.title: WCAG 2.1 Level AA Compliance
docs.text: As of January 20, 2026, CareConnect aims to meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards.

docs.title: Perceivable
docs.text:
• [x] Text Alternatives: All images have descriptive alt text or are marked as decorative (1.1.1).
  • [x] Time-based Media: N/A (The platform does not currently host video or audio content).
  • [x] Adaptable: Content structure is independent of presentation, using proper HTML5 semantics (landmarks, headings, lists) (1.3.1).
  • [x] Distinguishable: Color contrast ratios meet or exceed 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text (1.4.3).

docs.title: Operable
docs.text:
• [x] Keyboard Accessible: All functionality is available via keyboard interface (Tab, Enter, Space, Arrow keys) (2.1.1).
  • [x] No Keyboard Trap: Focus can be moved to and away from all components (2.1.2).
  • [x] Enough Time: No time-limited content exists (2.2.1).
  • [x] Seizures: No content flashes more than three times in any one second period (2.3.1).
  • [x] Navigable: Skip links are provided to bypass repetitive blocks (2.4.1). Focus order is logical and preserves meaning (2.4.3). Focus indicators are visible (2.4.7).

docs.title: Understandable
docs.text:
• [x] Readable: The language of the page can be explicitly determined (3.1.1). Content is written in plain language.
  • [x] Predictable: Navigation mechanisms are consistent across the site (3.2.3).
  • [x] Input Assistance: Labels or instructions are provided when content requires user input (3.3.2). Error identification is clear (3.3.1).

docs.title: Robust
docs.text:
• [x] Compatible: Parsing is robust, and ARIA attributes are used correctly to maximize compatibility with current and future user agents, including assistive technologies (4.1.1).
  • [x] Name, Role, Value: Review of all interactive elements ensures proper accessible names and roles (4.1.2).

docs.title: Evaluation Methods
docs.text:
We utilized the following methods to ensure compliance:

  1. Automated Testing:

    • @axe-core/playwright audits integrated into CI/CD pipelines.
    • eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y for static code analysis.

  2. Manual Testing:

    • Keyboard navigation verification (Tab order, focus traps).
    • Screen reader simulation.
    • Color contrast analysis using high-contrast mode testing.

docs.title: Support
docs.text:
If you encounter any accessibility barriers on CareConnect, please contact our support team. We are committed to resolving issues promptly.

Email: accessibility@careconnect.ing

docs.title: 1. docs/planning/archive/2026-01-25-v17-6-post-v17-5-enhancements.md ✅
docs.text:
29 pages of detailed implementation planning for v17.6+ follow-up work

Phase 1: Load Testing Baseline Establishment (2-3 hours)

  • Run all 4 load tests (smoke, search-api, sustained, spike)
  • Establish baseline metrics with regression detection thresholds
  • Document in docs/testing/baseline-metrics.md

Phase 2: Integration Tests (3-4 hours)

  • CI workflow for integration tests

Phase 3: French Translation Helper (2-3 hours)

  • NPM scripts: translate:prompt, translate:parse, translate:validate

Phase 4: Authorization Resilience (1-2 hours analysis)

  • 5 solution options with detailed pros/cons

docs.title: Keyboard Navigation Guide
docs.text: CareConnect is designed to be fully navigable via keyboard, adhering to WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards.

docs.title: Basic Navigation
docs.text:
• Tab: Move to the next interactive element (link, button, form field).
  • Shift + Tab: Move to the previous interactive element.
  • Enter: Activate a link or button.
  • Space: Toggle checkboxes or activate buttons.
  • Esc: Close modals, dialogs, or mobile menus.
  • Arrow Keys:
  • Navigate within radio groups.
  • Scroll through long content.
  • Navigate search suggestions (if applicable).

docs.title: Skip to Main Content
docs.text: When you first land on a page and press Tab, the first focusable element is the "Skip to main content" link. Pressing Enter while focused on this link will jump your focus directly to the primary content area, bypassing the header and navigation menu.

docs.title: Focus Indicators
docs.text: Every interactive element has a visible, high-contrast focus indicator (a colored ring or outline) when navigated via keyboard. If you ever lose track of your focus position, press Tab or Shift + Tab to relocate yourself.

docs.title: Modals & Dialogs
docs.text:
When a modal (like the Emergency Modal or Feedback Modal) opens:

  1. Focus is automatically trapped within the modal.
  2. You cannot Tab out of the modal to the background content.
  3. Pressing Esc or activating the Close button will return focus to the element that originally triggered the modal.

docs.title: Developer Guidelines
docs.text:
To maintain keyboard accessibility:

  1. Never suppress focus indicators: Do not use outline: none without providing a high-contrast focus-visible alternative.
  2. Use Semantic HTML: Use <button> for actions and <a> for navigation. Avoid using onClick on <div> or <span>.
  3. Logical Tab Order: Ensure the DOM order matches the visual layout to keep the Tab sequence predictable.
  4. ARIA Landmarks: Use <header>, <main>, <nav>, and <footer> correctly to help screen reader users navigate regions.

docs.title: AGENTS.md Guidelines
docs.text:
A reference for creating and maintaining effective AGENTS.md files for AI coding assistants.

Scope: This document provides general-purpose guidance applicable to any project's AGENTS.md file. It intentionally contains no project-specific details. All examples use fictional/generic codebases to illustrate patterns. When applying these guidelines, adapt the examples to your specific project's needs.

Based on analysis of 2,500+ repositories and industry best practices.

docs.title: Quick Reference
docs.text:
Essential sections (minimum viable AGENTS.md):

  1. Agent Role – Who the agent is, with prioritized principles
  2. Tech Stack – Technologies with versions
  3. Key Commands – Full syntax with flags
  4. Boundaries – Always / Ask First / Never

Most common mistakes:

  • Vague persona ("helpful assistant")
  • Commands without flags
  • No boundaries section
  • Prose instead of scannable lists/tables

Rule of thumb: If you'd repeat it across sessions, put it in AGENTS.md.

docs.title: Core Principles
docs.text:
1. Specialist over generalist – Define a specific role, not "helpful coding assistant"
  2. Show, don't tell – Code examples beat prose explanations
  3. Actionable over descriptive – Commands with flags, not theoretical descriptions
  4. Boundaries are critical – Clear Always/Ask First/Never rules prevent mistakes
  5. Living document – Update when patterns change; tell the agent to "update AGENTS.md with..."
  6. Stay in sync – The file should reflect actual practices, not aspirational ones

docs.title: File Naming & Compatibility
docs.text:
Several naming conventions exist across different AI tools:

File Primary Tool Notes AGENTS.md GitHub Copilot, Codex, Factory Emerging standard; broad compatibility CLAUDE.md Claude Code Anthropic's implementation CODEX.md OpenAI Codex (legacy) Transitioning to AGENTS.md .cursorrules Cursor Tool-specific format

Recommendation: Use AGENTS.md for broadest compatibility across tools. If using Claude Code specifically, you can symlink CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md to support both.

docs.title: Recommended Structure
docs.text:
# AGENTS.md – [Project Name]

## Agent Role
[Persona + priorities - what the agent IS, not just what the project is]

## Project Overview
[Mission, philosophy, tech stack with versions]

## Key Commands
[Executable commands with flags, grouped by category]

## Architecture
[System design, data flow, key patterns with code examples]

## Code Style & Conventions
[Real examples of good patterns, not just rules]

## Testing Strategy
[Framework, coverage requirements, expectations]

## Boundaries
[Three-tier: Always / Ask First / Never]

## Critical Files
[Entry points and key files to understand]

## Common Pitfalls
[Symptom → Cause → Fix format]

Not every section is required. Start with Agent Role, Tech Stack, Commands, and Boundaries, then expand as needed.

docs.title: Agent Role
docs.text:
Define a specialist persona with ordered priorities. The agent should know what to optimize for when trade-offs arise.

Good:

You are a **security-conscious backend developer** working on a payment processing API. Your priorities:
1. Security over convenience
2. Explicit error handling
3. Comprehensive logging
4. Performance (but never at the expense of security)

Bad:

You are a helpful coding assistant that helps with this project.

Why it matters: When an agent faces a choice between a quick fix and a secure fix, the prioritized list tells it which to choose.

docs.title: Tech Stack
docs.text:
Use a table with explicit versions. Agents often assume the latest version, which can cause compatibility issues.

Good:

Layer Technology Version Framework Express 4.x Language TypeScript 5.x Runtime Node.js 20+ Database PostgreSQL 15 ORM Prisma 5.x

Bad:

We use Express, TypeScript, and Node.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
Include full syntax with flags. Group by category (dev, test, build, deploy).

Good:

# Development
npm run dev              # Start with hot reload (port 3000)
npm run dev:debug        # Start with Node inspector attached

# Testing
npm test                 # Run all unit tests
npm test -- --watch      # Watch mode
npm run test:coverage    # With coverage report (threshold: 80%)
npm run test:e2e         # Playwright E2E tests

# Build & Deploy
npm run build            # Production build
npm run lint             # ESLint (must pass before commit)
npm run lint -- --fix    # Auto-fix lint issues

Bad:

Run tests with npm test. You can also run coverage tests.

docs.title: Boundaries (Three-Tier)
docs.text:
The most impactful section. Prevents costly mistakes and clarifies decision authority.

### ✅ Always
- Run `npm run lint` and `npm run type-check` before committing
- Write tests for new functionality
- Use the project's logger instead of console.log
- Handle errors explicitly (no silent catches)
- Update types when changing data structures

### ⚠️ Ask First
- Database schema changes (migrations)
- Changes to authentication or authorization logic
- Adding new dependencies (especially large ones)
- Removing or skipping tests
- Changing public API contracts
- Modifying CI/CD configuration

### 🚫 Never
- Commit, view, or access secrets, API keys, or `.env` files
- Disable or bypass security checks
- Force push to main/master
- Skip pre-commit hooks (`--no-verify`)
- Delete or modify production data directly
- Merge without passing CI

Tip: After an agent makes a mistake, add a corresponding boundary to prevent recurrence.

docs.title: Code Examples
docs.text:
Show real patterns. An example is worth a thousand words of explanation.

Good:

**Error handling** – Always use the Result pattern:
```typescript
// Good: Explicit error handling
const result = await fetchUser(id)
if (result.error) {
  logger.error("Failed to fetch user", { id, error: result.error })
  return { error: "User not found" }
}
return { data: result.data }
```

Bad:

Use proper error handling in all functions.

docs.title: Common Pitfalls
docs.text:
Use a table for quick scanning. Include the fix, not just the problem.

Symptom Likely Cause Fix Tests timeout Missing mock for external service Add mock in test setup Build fails on CI Works locally Check Node version matches CI Type errors after pull Schema changed Run npm run generate Auth redirects loop Cookie not set Check HTTPS/secure cookie settings

docs.title: What Belongs Where
docs.text:
Content Type AGENTS.md Prompt Build/test/lint commands ✓ Project architecture ✓ Code style conventions ✓ Repeatable workflows ✓ Security boundaries ✓ One-off task instructions ✓ Session-specific context ✓ Temporary debugging needs ✓ Bug ticket details ✓ Experimental approaches ✓

Persistence test: Would you want this instruction to apply to every future session? If yes → AGENTS.md. If no → prompt.

Pollution warning: Adding one-off instructions to AGENTS.md creates noise and can mislead agents in future sessions.

docs.title: Anti-Patterns to Avoid
docs.text: Anti-Pattern Problem Fix Vague persona Agent doesn't know priorities Define specific role + ordered priorities Commands without flags Agent guesses options Include full syntax with common flags Prose-only rules Hard to scan, easy to miss Use tables, lists, three-tier boundaries No code examples Agent invents patterns Show real examples from codebase Missing versions Agent assumes latest Table with explicit versions Overly long file Agent loses focus Keep under 500 lines; link to docs No boundaries Agent makes risky changes Always include boundaries section Stale examples Agent follows outdated patterns Review quarterly; update after refactors Conflicting instructions Agent gets confused Audit for contradictions Duplicated docs Drift between sources Single source of truth; link don't copy

docs.title: Hierarchy & Monorepos
docs.text:
AGENTS.md supports hierarchical placement. The closest file to the code being edited takes precedence.

repo/
├── AGENTS.md              # Root: org-wide standards
├── packages/
│   ├── api/
│   │   └── AGENTS.md      # API-specific: auth patterns, DB conventions
│   ├── web/
│   │   └── AGENTS.md      # Frontend-specific: component patterns, state
│   └── shared/
│       └── AGENTS.md      # Shared lib: export conventions, versioning

Root file: General standards (commit conventions, CI requirements, code style) Package files: Technology-specific patterns, local commands, package-specific boundaries

Nested files don't need to repeat root guidance—agents read up the tree.

docs.title: When to Update
docs.text: Trigger Action Agent made a preventable mistake Add to Boundaries (Never or Ask First) New pattern established Add code example Dependency upgraded Update version in Tech Stack New command added Add to Commands with flags Refactored architecture Update Architecture section Onboarded new team member Note what confused them Quarterly review Remove stale content, verify examples still work

docs.title: Maintenance Tips
docs.text:
1. Review quarterly – Remove stale patterns, verify examples compile
  2. Update after incidents – Every agent mistake is a missing boundary
  3. Keep it DRY – Link to detailed docs instead of duplicating
  4. Version in git – Track changes to understand what guidance evolved
  5. Test your examples – Stale code examples actively mislead
  6. Ask agents to update it – "Please add this pattern to AGENTS.md" builds institutional knowledge
  7. Read your own file – If you can't scan it quickly, neither can the agent

docs.title: Minimal Template
docs.text:
For new projects, start with this and expand as patterns emerge:

# AGENTS.md – [Project Name]

## Agent Role

You are a [specific role] working on [project type]. Your priorities:

1. [Most important principle]
2. [Second priority]
3. [Third priority]

## Tech Stack

| Layer     | Technology | Version |
| --------- | ---------- | ------- |
| Language  |            |         |
| Framework |            |         |
| Database  |            |         |

## Key Commands

```bash
npm run dev      # Start development server
npm test         # Run tests
npm run lint     # Lint code
npm run build    # Production build
```

docs.title: ✅ Always
docs.text:
• Run linting before committing
  • [Add project-specific requirements]

docs.title: ⚠️ Ask First
docs.text:
• Database schema changes
  • Adding new dependencies

docs.title: 🚫 Never
docs.text:
• Commit secrets or .env files
  • Force push to main

docs.title: Critical Files
docs.text:
• src/index.ts – Application entry point
  • [Add key files as project grows]
---

## References

- [agents.md specification](https://agents.md/) – Official format documentation
- [Instruction Files Overview](https://aruniyer.github.io/blog/agents-md-instruction-files.html) – Comparison of formats

docs.title: Tech Stack
docs.text:
• Framework: Next.js 15 (App Router)
  • Language: TypeScript
  • Styling: Tailwind CSS v4 + Radix UI
  • Database: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Vector)
  • Internationalization: next-intl (Support for EN, FR, AR, ZH-Hans, ES, PT, PA)
  • Testing: Playwright (E2E), Vitest (Unit)

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text:
• app/: Next.js App Router pages and API routes.
  • [locale]/: Localized routes.
  • api/v1/: RESTful API endpoints. (See OpenAPI Spec)
  • [locale]/offline/: PWA offline page (Workbox navigation fallback targets /offline).
  • worker.ts: Semantic search Web Worker.
  • components/: UI components.
  • ui/: Standardized primitives (Button, etc.).
  • hooks/: Custom React hooks (useSearch, useServices).
  • lib/: Utility functions (API helpers, search logic).
  • types/: TypeScript definitions.
  • messages/: Localization dictionaries (EN, FR, AR, ZH-Hans, ES, PT, PA).
  • docs/: Project documentation.

docs.title: Search Architecture
docs.text:
The search system uses a hybrid approach:

graph TD
    A[User Query] --> B{Keyword Search}
    B -- Results Found --> C[Re-rank based on Relevance]
    B -- Low/No Results --> D[Fuzzy Search Suggestion]
    C --> E[Lazy Semantic Search Worker]
    E --> F[Neural Re-ranking]
    F --> G[Final Results Display]
    A --> H[Search API POST - v16.0 Hybrid]
    H --> I[Server-side Hybrid Scoring]
    I --> G
  1. Instant Keyword Search: Filters results locally/via basic db queries for immediate feedback.
  2. Fuzzy Search ("Did you mean?"): If results are low, the Levenshtein algorithm suggests alternative queries based on service names and tags.
  3. Lazy Semantic Search: Loads the optional browser embedding worker in the background and upgrades local search with vector similarity only after the model initializes successfully. If worker initialization or embedding generation fails, the app fails closed to keyword-only search instead of emitting synthetic vectors.
  4. Search API (v16.0 Enhancements): A server-side alternative (POST /api/v1/search/services) that implements complex ranking factors including authority tiers, data completeness boosts, intent targeting, and continuous proximity decay. It uses a hybrid strategy: fetching the full filtered candidate set from the DB and scoring it in-memory using the same TypeScript pipeline as local mode so synthetic-query and intent-only matches are preserved. The shared request contract now carries category, location, and openNow filters in both modes, including empty-query "open now" browsing.
  5. Governance Freshness Enforcement: Both local and server search exclude records that fall outside the 180-day public-visibility window so stale listings do not keep ranking with only a soft penalty.
  6. Result Explainability: Public result cards and linked detail pages can surface normalized match reasons so users can inspect why a service ranked for their query.

docs.title: Search Modes
docs.text:
The application supports two search modes, controlled by NEXT_PUBLIC_SEARCH_MODE:

  1. Local (Default): Downloads a compressed JSON bundle of all services. Search logic runs entirely in the browser. Best for offline support and zero-latency typing.
  2. Server: Sends POST requests to the Librarian API. The server executes the query and returns results. Best for large datasets (>1000 items) and devices with limited RAM. (Note: Server mode does not load the JSON bundle, saving bandwidth).

docs.title: AI Assistant Architecture
docs.text:
• Engine: @mlc-ai/web-llm (WebGPU) running in a Web Worker for UI responsiveness.
  • Strategy: "LLM-as-Search" (query rewrite/expansion) + deterministic rendering of search results.
  • Privacy:
  • Local-Only: Inference runs entirely in the user's browser.
  • No Data Egress: Queries never leave the device.
  • Zero-Knowledge: Server knows that a user is chatting, but not what they are saying.
  • Zero-Logging Search (v13.0): When using Server Search, queries are sent as POST (no URL logs). Query-, location-, and open-now-driven responses are Cache-Control: no-store; only anonymous category-only browse responses may use short public caching. The database services_public view enforces a strict data boundary.
  • Share Target Handoff: POST /api/v1/share stores shared search text in a short-lived first-party cookie and redirects without ?q= so sensitive share text does not land in URLs, history, or referrers.
  • Lifecycle:
  • Opt-In: Model download only triggered by explicit user action.
  • Idle Cleanup: VRAM released after 5 minutes of inactivity.

docs.title: Privacy-Preserving Personalization
docs.text:
• Client-Side Profile: User demographics (Age, Identities) stored in localStorage (careconnect_user_context).
  • Zero PII: No user accounts, login, or cookies required for basic personalization.
  • Local Eligibility: "Likely Qualify" checks run locally by parsing cached service data against the local profile.
  • Identity Boosting: Search ranking adjustments happen on the client-side WebWorker.

docs.title: Data Pipelines
docs.text:
• Source of Truth: Manually curated service records remain authoritative. In development they live in data/services.json; in production the app reads from Supabase when configured, with local JSON fallback when the database is unavailable.
  • DB-Authoritative Runtime Reads: When Supabase is available, runtime search/detail loading now reads directly from the database contract instead of overlaying live search metadata from local JSON snapshots. Curated JSON remains the no-DB/offline fallback and the import source for backfills. Existing environments must run the rollout-safe curated backfill once after the contract change so blank DB runtime fields are populated without overwriting live values.
sequenceDiagram
    participant Curators as Curators + Research Inputs
    participant Import as Validation + Import Scripts
    participant DB as Verified Database
    participant Embed as Embedding Generator

    Curators->>Import: Verified JSON / CSV / seed files
    Import->>Import: Normalize & validate schema
    Import->>DB: Upsert curated records
    DB->>Embed: Pull Verified Listings
    Embed->>DB: Store Vector Embeddings
    DB->>Client: JSON/API Delivery
  • Ingestion:
  • Human-reviewed JSON, CSV, and municipal/provider seed files are validated before import.
  • scripts/import/geojson-import.ts: Generic utility for ingesting municipal (City of Kingston) and specialized (Indigenous/Faith) seed files.
  • generate-embeddings.ts: Generates logical-semantic embeddings at build time.
  • Versioning: generate-changelog.ts tracks diffs between syncs.
  • Offline Export Contract: /api/v1/services/export sanitizes the public payload, derives a stable SHA-256 fingerprint for both version and ETag, and offline sync clears the in-memory service cache after a successful refresh so newly synced data is visible immediately.

docs.title: User Feedback & Impact Loop (v14.0)
docs.text:
• Architecture: Privacy-preserving feedback system.
  • Pipeline: Client (FeedbackWidget) -> API (/api/v1/feedback) -> Supabase (feedback table).
  • Aggregations: Database materialized views (feedback_aggregations, unmet_needs_summary) provide performant metrics for the /impact page and Partner Dashboard.
  • Privacy: No PII, cookies, or persistent IDs are stored. Rate limiting is handled in-memory.

docs.title: Equity-First Access (v14.0)
docs.text:
• Localization Parity: Full UI translation coverage for all 7 supported locales. Mandatory EN/FR parity for all service data.
  • Simplified Views: Optional plain-language summaries (Grade 6-8 reading level) for high-impact services, stored in plain_language_summaries.
  • Low-Bandwidth Outputs: Printable "Resource Cards" optimized for physical distribution and accessibility.
  • Printable Card Privacy: Printable cards escape interpolated service content and render QR codes locally as inline data URLs instead of calling third-party QR hosts.

docs.title: Visible Verification & Trust Signals (v14.0)
docs.text:
• Provenance: TrustPanel displays last_verified dates, verification methods, and source provenance to build user confidence.
  • Verification Levels:
  • L1: Basic verification.
  • L2: Manual verification by CareConnect team.
  • L3: Partner-claimed and verified.
  • L4: Governance-level policy concept for future partner/audit workflows; not currently represented in runtime types or search scoring.
  • Partner Update Workflow: Structured request-approval loop via service_update_requests ensures data integrity while engaging service providers.

docs.title: Push Notifications
docs.text:
• Technology: Web Push API + Service Worker (public/sw.js, plus public/custom-sw.js for app-specific hooks).
  • Flow: User Opt-In -> Service Worker Subscribes -> Endpoint stored in push_subscriptions -> Server-side trigger via VAPID keys.
  • Privacy: No PII linked to subscriptions. User can revoked at any time via browser settings.

docs.title: Automated Maintenance Bots
docs.text:
• URL Health Bot: Monthly check of all service URLs (scripts/health-check-urls.ts). When a provider's public page is bot-hostile from CI, the checker can fall back to a small allowlist of official provider override probes without changing the curated public URL stored in service data.
  • Phone Validator: Connectivity checks using Twilio Lookup API (scripts/validate-phones.ts).
  • Automation: GitHub Actions maintain quiet-by-default governance issues and prefer job summaries over routine PR comments.
  • Finding Workflows: Health and staleness workflows reuse one bot issue per lane, update it while action is needed, and close it automatically after the condition clears. The monthly URL health workflow also writes a readable summary of broken and inconclusive checks into the Actions run.
  • Reminder Workflows: Monthly/quarterly verification reminders reuse one issue per lane, reopen it for the new cycle, and keep a compact recent-cycle history instead of opening a new issue every run.

docs.title: Database Security & Row Level Security (RLS)
docs.text:
• Security Model: Supabase PostgreSQL with Row Level Security ensures data isolation and privacy.
  • Public Views: services_public view created with security_invoker = true to use invoker's permissions (not definer's).
  • Hardened Policies: All INSERT policies validate foreign keys (e.g., service_id IN (SELECT id FROM services_public)) to prevent spam and invalid data.
  • Performance Optimizations: Auth function calls wrapped in scalar subqueries (SELECT auth.uid()) to avoid per-row re-evaluation.
  • Policy Consolidation: Separate policies for SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE to avoid "Multiple Permissive Policies" performance warnings.
  • Public Transparency: Aggregated metrics are exposed via Standard Views (feedback_aggregations, unmet_needs_summary) that wrap underlying Materialized Views (mat_*). These wrappers enforce security_invoker = true to ensure the query runs with the user's permissions, satisfying security audits while maintaining API compatibility.
  • Documentation: See Database Security Guide for complete RLS policy documentation.
  • Testing: RLS policies verified via tests/integration/rls-policies.test.ts.

docs.title: Partner Dashboard & RBAC
docs.text:
• Access Control: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) implemented via organization_members table.
  • Roles: owner, admin, editor, viewer.
  • Functions: CRUD operations for listings, member invites (invite/accept flow), and analytics viewing.
  • Multi-Lingual Content: Self-service editing for English and French fields (local services are EN/FR only).
  • Mutation Guardrails: Partner-facing write APIs use an explicit allowlist for editable fields. owner and admin can mutate any service in their organization, editor is limited to compatible ownership signals on services they created, and viewer is denied. Direct partner PUT/PATCH writes now persist access_script and access_script_fr through the typed service storage contract while still rejecting privileged/unknown fields.

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.text:
• Services: Fetched via /api/v1/services. Cached using SWR-like strategies in hooks.
  • Analytics: Aggregate-only search events (locale + resultCount) are posted to /api/v1/analytics/search asynchronously. Raw query text, category, and location are not stored.

docs.title: v22 Phase 0 Pilot Instrumentation (Internal)
docs.text:
• Purpose: Capture pilot-only connection outcome signals before Phase 1 feature rollout.
  • Internal Endpoints:
  • POST /api/v1/pilot/events/contact-attempt
  • POST /api/v1/pilot/events/connection
  • POST /api/v1/pilot/events/referral
  • POST /api/v1/pilot/events/service-status
  • POST /api/v1/pilot/events/data-decay-audit
  • POST /api/v1/pilot/events/preference-fit
  • PATCH /api/v1/pilot/events/referral/{id}
  • POST /api/v1/pilot/scope/services
  • POST /api/v1/pilot/metrics/recompute
  • POST /api/v1/pilot/integration-feasibility
  • GET /api/v1/pilot/metrics/scorecard
  • Security Model:
  • Authenticated session required for all pilot endpoints.
  • Organization-scoped authorization enforced for event writes and scorecard reads.
  • Admin authorization required for integration feasibility decision writes.
  • Privacy Constraints:
  • Request payload validation rejects disallowed keys (query, query_text, message, user_text, notes).
  • Raw query text is never accepted into pilot event contracts.
  • Repeat-failure attribution uses opaque entity_key_hash values rather than raw identifiers.
  • Resilience:
  • Pilot DB operations use circuit-breaker-wrapped storage access.
  • If pilot tables are not present, endpoints fail with explicit 501 responses to prevent silent data loss.
  • Metric recompute writes canonical snapshots to pilot_metric_snapshots; scorecard reads stay snapshot-based.
  • Readiness Reporting:
  • pilot_service_scope is the canonical scoped-service source for bounded pilot readiness audits.
  • npm run audit:pilot-readiness -- --pilot-cycle-id <id> [--org-id <uuid>] exports scoped JSON, Markdown, and CSV artifacts without mutating curated service data.
  • docs/implementation/v22-pilot-readiness/ contains the scope-file-first handoff bundle, including the runbook and scope.template.json for cases where a committed pilot cycle does not yet exist.

docs.title: Routing & Discovery
docs.text:
• Public Routes:
  • /service/[id]: Rich detail page.
  • /submit-service: Public crowdsourcing form.
  • /dashboard: Partner portal.
  • /about: Project mission and impact metrics. Includes Katarokwi Land Acknowledgment.
  • /about/partners: Data source transparency and verification process.
  • Provincial Services Discovery:
  • is_provincial flag allows services to be promoted in search results globally.
  • Provincial services display a "Province-Wide" badge for clarity.
  • Language Selection:
  • LanguageSelector component in Header provides 7-locale switching.
  • Arabic triggers RTL (Right-to-Left) direction in root layout.
  • Internal Links: ServiceCard now links to internal detail pages instead of external URLs.

docs.title: Partner Claim Workflow
docs.text:
• Claim Logic: Unclaimed services can be claimed by authenticated organizations.
  • Verification: Claiming a service automatically elevates its status to L1.
  • Atomic Operations: lib/services.ts handles the claim logic with database consistency checks.

docs.title: Hooks Architecture
docs.text:
We use a modular hook system to separate concerns:

  • Search Hooks: useSearch coordindates state, useServices handles logic, and useSemanticSearch manages the worker.
  • Utility Hooks: Generic hooks for localStorage and Geolocation ensure SSR safety and consistency.

docs.title: Logging & Monitoring
docs.text:
• Logger Utility: Located in lib/logger.ts. Use instead of console.log.
  • Error IDs: The Error Boundary generates unique IDs (e.g., ERR-K9X2J1) for cross-referencing logs with user reports.
  • Health Visibility: In production, /api/v1/health exposes only basic public status and has no SLO side effects. Detailed public-probe execution lives at /api/v1/health/probe, which requires Authorization: Bearer ${HEALTH_PROBE_TOKEN} and is the only path that records uptime samples and evaluates SLO alerts.

docs.title: User Interface & Accessibility
docs.text:
• High Contrast Mode: Global state managed via useHighContrast hook, applying .high-contrast class and CSS variable overrides.
  • Print Optimization: Specific @media print styles in globals.css and PrintButton component for physical delivery of information.
  • Data Freshness: FreshnessBadge provides visual cues on the reliability of data based on last_verified / provenance.verified_at, including an explicit expired state once a record crosses the 180-day governance limit.
  • Offline Safety Surfaces: OfflineSnapshotStatus reads IndexedDB sync metadata (lastSync) and shows snapshot age plus stale-data warnings on offline surfaces when cached data may be outdated.
  • External Maps: Service-detail pages keep Google Maps loading opt-in. Directions remain available, but third-party map previews do not load until the user explicitly requests them.
  • Search Explainability Surfaces: ServiceMatchReasons shows deduplicated match reasons on result cards and on detail pages when the user follows a search result with ranking context attached.

docs.title: Development
docs.text:
• npm run dev: Start local server.
  • npm run test: Run the default Vitest suite.
  • npm run test:db: Run real DB-backed retrieval and policy tests.
  • npm run test:e2e:local: Run partial E2E tests (CI handles full suite).
  • AGENTS.md: Contributor and local workflow guidance.
  • docs/development/testing-guidelines.md: Detailed testing guidance.

docs.title: Bilingual / Multi-Lingual Development Guide
docs.text:
This page is maintained in docs/development/bilingual-guide.md.

  • Canonical: docs/development/bilingual-guide.md

docs.title: Documentation Guidelines
docs.text:
This page is maintained in docs/governance/documentation-guidelines.md.

  • Canonical: docs/governance/documentation-guidelines.md

docs.title: Foire aux questions (FAQ)
docs.text: Dernière révision : 1 mai 2026

docs.title: Qu'est-ce que CareConnect ?
docs.text:
CareConnect est un moteur de recherche gratuit qui vous aide à trouver des services sociaux à Kingston, Ontario. Nous avons des informations sur 196 services vérifiés, y compris les banques alimentaires, le soutien en cas de crise, l'aide au logement, les services de santé, et plus encore.

C'est comme Google, mais uniquement pour les services sociaux à Kingston.

docs.title: Est-ce un service gouvernemental officiel ?
docs.text:
Non. CareConnect est un projet communautaire créé par des bénévoles. Nous ne sommes pas affiliés à :

  • Ville de Kingston
  • Province de l'Ontario
  • Gouvernement fédéral
  • Toute agence de services sociaux spécifique

Nous recueillons des informations publiquement disponibles et les rendons plus faciles à rechercher.

docs.title: Combien cela coûte-t-il ?
docs.text:
Complètement gratuit. Il n'y a pas de :

  • Frais
  • Abonnements
  • Publicités
  • Frais cachés

Nous ne demanderons jamais de paiement ou d'informations de carte de crédit.

docs.title: Dois-je créer un compte ?
docs.text:
Non. Vous pouvez rechercher sans :

  • Créer un compte
  • Donner votre nom
  • Partager votre courriel
  • Vous connecter

La plateforme est complètement anonyme.

docs.title: Comment signaler des informations incorrectes ?
docs.text:
Si vous trouvez des informations incorrectes ou périmées :

Envoyez-nous un courriel : feedback@careconnect.ing

Veuillez inclure :

  • Nom du service
  • Quelle information est incorrecte
  • Information correcte (si vous la connaissez)

Nous corrigeons généralement les problèmes dans les 2-3 jours ouvrables.

docs.title: Pourquoi [service spécifique] n'est-il pas listé ?
docs.text:
Nous n'avons peut-être pas listé un service pour plusieurs raisons :

  1. Nous ne le connaissons pas encore - Envoyez-nous un courriel pour le suggérer
  2. Il est en dehors de Kingston - Nous nous concentrons sur les services de Kingston
  3. Ce n'est pas un service social - Nous nous concentrons sur les services essentiels (nourriture, logement, santé, crise, etc.)
  4. Il a fermé ou fusionné - Nous mettons à jour nos dossiers

Pour suggérer un service : Envoyez un courriel à feedback@careconnect.ing avec le nom du service, ce qu'il fait et les coordonnées.

docs.title: Suivez-vous mes recherches ?
docs.text:
Non. Nous ne voyons jamais ce que vous recherchez.

Comment ça marche :

  • Toutes les recherches se font sur votre appareil (dans votre navigateur)
  • Vos termes de recherche n'atteignent jamais nos serveurs
  • Personne ne peut voir votre historique de recherche
  • Pas même nous

C'est ce qu'on appelle la « recherche côté client » et cela protège complètement votre confidentialité.

docs.title: Mes données sont-elles partagées avec quelqu'un ?
docs.text:
Non. Nous ne collectons pas vos données, donc nous ne pouvons pas les partager.

Ce que nous NE collectons PAS :

  • Vos recherches
  • Votre localisation
  • Votre nom ou courriel
  • Votre historique de navigation
  • Votre adresse IP (dans la plupart des cas)

Ce que nous utilisons :

  • Témoins techniques minimaux (pour faire fonctionner le site)
  • Préférence de langue (stockée uniquement dans votre navigateur)

docs.title: Les fournisseurs de services peuvent-ils voir qui les recherche ?
docs.text:
Non. Les fournisseurs de services ne peuvent pas voir :

  • Qui les a recherchés
  • Combien de personnes les ont recherchés
  • Quels termes de recherche les gens ont utilisés
  • Quand les gens ont consulté leurs informations

Toute activité de recherche est complètement privée.

docs.title: Comment puis-je vérifier que vous ne me suivez pas ?
docs.text:
Bonne question ! Voici comment vérifier :

  1. Code open source : Notre code est public sur github.com/jerdaw/careconnect
  2. Tout le monde peut l'examiner

  3. Des experts en sécurité ont vérifié nos revendications de confidentialité

  4. Outils de développement du navigateur :

  5. Appuyez sur F12 dans votre navigateur
  6. Allez dans l'onglet « Réseau »
  7. Recherchez quelque chose

  8. Vous verrez que les recherches n'envoient pas de données à nos serveurs

  9. Audit de confidentialité : Nous avons passé des audits de confidentialité tiers (documentation disponible sur demande)

docs.title: Comment vérifiez-vous les services ?
docs.text:
Nous utilisons un système de vérification à 3 niveaux :

Niveau 1 (N1) - Basique :

  • Nous avons confirmé que le service existe
  • Nous avons vérifié leur site Web ou numéro de téléphone
  • ⚠️ Les informations peuvent ne pas être à jour

Niveau 2 (N2) - Vérifié :

  • Nous avons contacté le service directement
  • Nous avons confirmé les heures, l'emplacement et l'éligibilité
  • ✅ Informations plus fiables

Niveau 3 (N3) - Certifié :

  • Partenariat officiel avec le service
  • Ils mettent à jour leurs propres informations
  • ✅ Informations les plus fiables

Recherchez le badge de vérification sur chaque carte de service.

docs.title: À quelle fréquence les informations sont-elles mises à jour ?
docs.text:
Calendrier de mise à jour :

  • Services prioritaires (crise, nourriture, logement) : Examinés tous les 3 mois
  • Autres services : Examinés tous les 6 mois
  • Rapports des utilisateurs : Corrigés dans les 2-3 jours ouvrables

Dernier audit complet : Nous notons ceci en haut de la page de détails de chaque service.

Si les informations semblent incorrectes : Veuillez les signaler afin que nous puissions les mettre à jour rapidement.

docs.title: Que signifient les niveaux de vérification ?
docs.text:
N1 (Vérification basique) :

  • ⚠️ Utiliser avec prudence
  • Appelez toujours d'abord pour confirmer les détails
  • Les informations peuvent être périmées

N2 (Vérifié) :

  • ✅ Bonne fiabilité
  • Nous avons confirmé les détails récemment
  • Appelez quand même pour confirmer l'éligibilité

N3 (Partenariat certifié) :

  • ✅ Meilleure fiabilité
  • Le service met à jour ses propres informations
  • Informations les plus à jour

Conseil : Préférez les services N2 et N3 lorsque possible.

docs.title: Puis-je ajouter un service moi-même ?
docs.text:
Pas directement. Pour protéger la qualité des données, nous examinons manuellement tous les nouveaux services.

Pour suggérer un service :

  1. Envoyez un courriel à feedback@careconnect.ing
  2. Incluez :
  3. Nom du service
  4. Ce qu'ils font
  5. Qui ils aident
  6. Téléphone, courriel, site Web

  7. Adresse (si emplacement physique)

  8. Nous examinerons dans 1 semaine et l'ajouterons si approprié.

docs.title: Quels navigateurs sont pris en charge ?
docs.text:
Entièrement pris en charge :

  • ✅ Chrome (toute version récente)
  • ✅ Firefox (toute version récente)
  • ✅ Safari (iOS 12+, macOS 10.14+)
  • ✅ Edge (version Chromium)

Non pris en charge :

  • ❌ Internet Explorer (toutes versions)
  • ❌ Navigateurs d'avant 2020

Si le site ne fonctionne pas :

  • Mettez à jour votre navigateur vers la dernière version
  • Essayez un autre navigateur (Chrome recommandé)

docs.title: Fonctionne-t-il hors ligne ?
docs.text:
Oui ! Le site fonctionne sans Internet après votre première visite.

Comment ça marche :

  1. Première visite (avec Internet) : Le site télécharge les données de service sur votre appareil
  2. Visites ultérieures (sans Internet) : La recherche fonctionne normalement en utilisant les données enregistrées
  3. Quand en ligne à nouveau : Les données se mettent à jour automatiquement

Ce qui fonctionne hors ligne :

  • ✅ Rechercher
  • ✅ Voir les détails du service
  • ✅ Lire les informations
  • ❌ Cliquer sur les liens de site Web
  • ❌ Envoyer des courriels
  • ❌ Directions de carte en direct

Cela aide si vous avez :

  • Internet lent
  • Données mobiles limitées
  • WiFi intermittent

docs.title: Y a-t-il une application mobile ?
docs.text:
Pas encore. Mais le site Web fonctionne très bien sur les téléphones :

Avantages du site Web :

  • Fonctionne sur n'importe quel téléphone (iPhone, Android)
  • Aucun téléchargement nécessaire
  • Pas de délais d'approbation de l'app store
  • Fonctionne hors ligne (voir ci-dessus)
  • Toujours à jour

Ajouter à votre écran d'accueil :

iPhone :

  1. Appuyez sur le bouton Partager
  2. Appuyez sur « Ajouter à l'écran d'accueil »
  3. Ça fonctionne comme une application

Android :

  1. Appuyez sur le menu (⋮)
  2. Appuyez sur « Ajouter à l'écran d'accueil »
  3. Ça fonctionne comme une application

docs.title: Que faire si j'ai des besoins en matière d'accessibilité ?
docs.text:
Nous avons conçu cette plateforme pour être accessible à tous.

Fonctionnalités pour les personnes handicapées :

Déficiences visuelles :

  • ✅ Fonctionne avec les lecteurs d'écran (JAWS, NVDA, VoiceOver)
  • ✅ Support de contraste élevé
  • ✅ Le texte peut être agrandi (zoom du navigateur)
  • ✅ Navigation au clavier

Handicaps moteurs :

  • ✅ Navigation complète au clavier (pas de souris nécessaire)
  • ✅ Grandes cibles de clic
  • ✅ Option de mouvement réduit

Handicaps cognitifs :

  • ✅ Langage simple (niveau de lecture de 8e année)
  • ✅ En-têtes et structure clairs
  • ✅ Navigation prévisible

Déficiences auditives :

  • ✅ Pas de contenu uniquement audio
  • ✅ Toutes les informations sont textuelles

Si vous trouvez un problème d'accessibilité : Envoyez un courriel à feedback@careconnect.ing et nous le corrigerons rapidement.

docs.title: Pourquoi demande-t-il ma localisation ?
docs.text:
Réponse courte : Pour vous montrer les services près de chez vous, triés par distance.

Comment ça marche :

  1. Vous cliquez sur « Utiliser ma localisation »
  2. Votre navigateur demande la permission
  3. Si vous l'autorisez, les résultats sont triés par distance
  4. Votre localisation ne quitte jamais votre appareil
  5. Nous ne voyons ni ne stockons votre localisation

Vous pouvez toujours dire non.

  • La recherche fonctionne bien sans localisation
  • Vous ne verrez simplement pas le tri par distance

Garantie de confidentialité :

  • Votre localisation est uniquement utilisée pour le tri
  • Elle n'est pas envoyée à nos serveurs
  • Elle n'est pas sauvegardée
  • Elle n'est partagée avec personne

docs.title: Puis-je changer la langue ?
docs.text:
Oui ! Nous prenons en charge 7 langues :

  1. English (Anglais)
  2. Français
  3. 中文 (Chinois)
  4. العربية (Arabe)
  5. Português (Portugais)
  6. Español (Espagnol)
  7. ਪੰਜਾਬੀ (Pendjabi)

Pour changer de langue :

  1. Regardez dans le coin supérieur droit
  2. Cliquez sur le menu déroulant de langue
  3. Sélectionnez votre langue
  4. Le site entier bascule

Remarque : Les descriptions de service peuvent être uniquement en anglais et en français (dépend de ce que le service a fourni).

docs.title: Que faire si je suis en crise ?
docs.text:
Si vous êtes en danger immédiat, appelez le 911.

Pour une crise de santé mentale :

  • Appelez ou textez le 988 (Ligne canadienne de prévention du suicide)
  • Centre de détresse Kingston : 613-544-1771 (24/7)

Pour la violence domestique :

  • Interval House : 613-546-1833 (24/7)

La plateforme vous montrera des ressources de crise si vous recherchez des mots comme :

  • « suicide »
  • « abus »
  • « crise »
  • « aidez-moi »

docs.title: Puis-je utiliser ceci pour signaler des abus ?
docs.text:
Non. Ceci est un outil de recherche, pas un système de signalement.

Pour signaler :

  • Abus d'enfants : Appelez la Société d'aide à l'enfance ou le 911
  • Abus de personnes âgées : Appelez la police (613-549-4660) ou le 911
  • Violence domestique : Appelez Interval House (613-546-1833) ou le 911

La plateforme vous aide à TROUVER des services, pas à leur signaler.

docs.title: Vous avez encore des questions ?
docs.text:
Contactez-nous :

  • Courriel : feedback@careconnect.ing
  • GitHub : github.com/jerdaw/careconnect/issues

Temps de réponse : Généralement 2-3 jours ouvrables

Merci d'utiliser CareConnect !

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
docs.text: Last reviewed: May 1, 2026

docs.title: What is CareConnect?
docs.text:
CareConnect is a free search engine that helps you find social services in Kingston, Ontario. We have information on 196 verified services including food banks, crisis support, housing help, health services, and more.

It's like Google, but only for social services in Kingston.

docs.title: Is this an official government service?
docs.text:
No. CareConnect is a community project created by volunteers. We are not affiliated with:

  • City of Kingston
  • Province of Ontario
  • Federal government
  • Any specific social service agency

We gather publicly available information and make it easier to search.

docs.title: How much does it cost?
docs.text:
Completely free. There are no:

  • Fees
  • Subscriptions
  • Ads
  • Hidden charges

We will never ask for payment or credit card information.

docs.title: Do I need to create an account?
docs.text:
No. You can search without:

  • Creating an account
  • Giving your name
  • Sharing your email
  • Logging in

The platform is completely anonymous.

docs.title: How do I report incorrect information?
docs.text:
If you find wrong or outdated information:

Email us: feedback@careconnect.ing

Please include:

  • Name of the service
  • What information is wrong
  • Correct information (if you know it)

We typically fix issues within 2-3 business days.

docs.title: Why isn't [specific service] listed?
docs.text:
We might not have listed a service for several reasons:

  1. We don't know about it yet - Email us to suggest it
  2. It's outside Kingston - We focus on Kingston services
  3. It's not a social service - We focus on essential services (food, housing, health, crisis, etc.)
  4. It closed or merged - We're updating our records

To suggest a service: Email feedback@careconnect.ing with the service name, what they do, and contact information.

docs.title: Do you track my searches?
docs.text:
No. We never see what you search for.

How it works:

  • All searches happen on your device (in your browser)
  • Your search terms never reach our servers
  • No one can see your search history
  • Not even us

This is called "client-side search" and protects your privacy completely.

docs.title: Is my data shared with anyone?
docs.text:
No. We don't collect your data, so we can't share it.

What we DON'T collect:

  • Your searches
  • Your location
  • Your name or email
  • Your browsing history
  • Your IP address (in most cases)

What we DO use:

  • Minimal technical cookies (to make the site work)
  • Language preference (stored in your browser only)

docs.title: Can service providers see who searches for them?
docs.text:
No. Service providers cannot see:

  • Who searched for them
  • How many people searched for them
  • What search terms people used
  • When people viewed their information

All search activity is completely private.

docs.title: How can I verify you don't track me?
docs.text:
Good question! Here's how to check:

  1. Open source code: Our code is public at github.com/jerdaw/careconnect
  2. Anyone can review it

  3. Security experts have verified our privacy claims

  4. Browser dev tools:

  5. Press F12 in your browser
  6. Go to "Network" tab
  7. Search for something

  8. You'll see searches don't send data to our servers

  9. Privacy audit: We passed third-party privacy audits (documentation available on request)

docs.title: How do you verify services?
docs.text:
We use a 3-level verification system:

Level 1 (L1) - Basic:

  • We confirmed the service exists
  • We checked their website or phone number
  • ⚠️ Information may not be up-to-date

Level 2 (L2) - Vetted:

  • We contacted the service directly
  • We confirmed hours, location, and eligibility
  • ✅ More reliable information

Level 3 (L3) - Verified:

  • Official partnership with the service
  • They update their own information
  • ✅ Most reliable information

Look for the verification badge on each service card.

docs.title: How often is information updated?
docs.text:
Update schedule:

  • High-priority services (crisis, food, housing): Reviewed every 3 months
  • Other services: Reviewed every 6 months
  • User reports: Fixed within 2-3 business days

Last full audit: We note this at the top of each service's detail page.

If information seems wrong: Please report it so we can update it quickly.

docs.title: What do verification levels mean?
docs.text:
L1 (Basic Verification):

  • ⚠️ Use with caution
  • Always call first to confirm details
  • Information may be outdated

L2 (Vetted):

  • ✅ Good reliability
  • We've confirmed details recently
  • Still call to confirm eligibility

L3 (Verified Partnership):

  • ✅ Best reliability
  • Service updates their own info
  • Most current information

Tip: Prefer L2 and L3 services when possible.

docs.title: Can I add a service myself?
docs.text:
Not directly. To protect data quality, we manually review all new services.

To suggest a service:

  1. Email feedback@careconnect.ing
  2. Include:
  3. Service name
  4. What they do
  5. Who they help
  6. Phone, email, website

  7. Address (if physical location)

  8. We'll review within 1 week and add if appropriate.

docs.title: Which browsers are supported?
docs.text:
Fully supported:

  • ✅ Chrome (any recent version)
  • ✅ Firefox (any recent version)
  • ✅ Safari (iOS 12+, macOS 10.14+)
  • ✅ Edge (Chromium version)

Not supported:

  • ❌ Internet Explorer (all versions)
  • ❌ Browsers from before 2020

If the site doesn't work:

  • Update your browser to the latest version
  • Try a different browser (Chrome recommended)

docs.title: Does it work offline?
docs.text:
Yes! The site works without internet after your first visit.

How it works:

  1. First visit (with internet): The site downloads service data to your device
  2. Later visits (without internet): Search works normally using saved data
  3. When online again: Data updates automatically

What works offline:

  • ✅ Searching
  • ✅ Viewing service details
  • ✅ Reading information
  • ❌ Clicking website links
  • ❌ Sending emails
  • ❌ Live map directions

This helps if you have:

  • Slow internet
  • Limited mobile data
  • Spotty WiFi

docs.title: Is there a mobile app?
docs.text:
Not yet. But the website works great on phones:

Benefits of the website:

  • Works on any phone (iPhone, Android)
  • No download needed
  • No app store approval delays
  • Works offline (see above)
  • Always up-to-date

Add to your home screen:

iPhone:

  1. Tap the Share button
  2. Tap "Add to Home Screen"
  3. It works like an app

Android:

  1. Tap the menu (⋮)
  2. Tap "Add to Home Screen"
  3. It works like an app

docs.title: What if I have accessibility needs?
docs.text:
We designed this platform to be accessible to everyone.

Features for people with disabilities:

Visual impairments:

  • ✅ Works with screen readers (JAWS, NVDA, VoiceOver)
  • ✅ High contrast support
  • ✅ Text can be enlarged (browser zoom)
  • ✅ Keyboard navigation

Motor disabilities:

  • ✅ Full keyboard navigation (no mouse needed)
  • ✅ Large click targets
  • ✅ Reduced motion option

Cognitive disabilities:

  • ✅ Simple language (Grade 8 reading level)
  • ✅ Clear headings and structure
  • ✅ Predictable navigation

Hearing impairments:

  • ✅ No audio-only content
  • ✅ All information is text-based

If you find an accessibility issue: Email feedback@careconnect.ing and we'll fix it quickly.

docs.title: Why does it ask for my location?
docs.text:
Short answer: To show you services near you, sorted by distance.

How it works:

  1. You click "Use my location"
  2. Your browser asks permission
  3. If you allow it, results are sorted by distance
  4. Your location never leaves your device
  5. We never see or store your location

You can always say no.

  • Search works fine without location
  • You just won't see distance sorting

Privacy guarantee:

  • Your location is only used for sorting
  • It's not sent to our servers
  • It's not saved
  • It's not shared with anyone

docs.title: Can I change the language?
docs.text:
Yes! We support 7 languages:

  1. English
  2. Français (French)
  3. 中文 (Chinese)
  4. العربية (Arabic)
  5. Português (Portuguese)
  6. Español (Spanish)
  7. ਪੰਜਾਬੀ (Punjabi)

To change language:

  1. Look at the top-right corner
  2. Click the language dropdown
  3. Select your language
  4. The entire site switches

Note: Service descriptions may only be in English and French (depends on what the service provided).

docs.title: What if I'm in crisis?
docs.text:
If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

For mental health crisis:

  • Call or text 988 (Canada Suicide Prevention Line)
  • Distress Centre Kingston: 613-544-1771 (24/7)

For domestic violence:

  • Interval House: 613-546-1833 (24/7)

The platform will show you crisis resources if you search for words like:

  • "suicide"
  • "abuse"
  • "crisis"
  • "help me"

docs.title: Can I use this to report abuse?
docs.text:
No. This is a search tool, not a reporting system.

To report:

  • Child abuse: Call Children's Aid Society or 911
  • Elder abuse: Call police (613-549-4660) or 911
  • Domestic violence: Call Interval House (613-546-1833) or 911

The platform helps you FIND services, not report to them.

docs.title: Still Have Questions?
docs.text:
Contact us:

  • Email: feedback@careconnect.ing
  • GitHub: github.com/jerdaw/careconnect/issues

Response time: Usually 2-3 business days

Thank you for using CareConnect!

docs.title: Testing Guidelines
docs.text:
This page is maintained in docs/development/testing-guidelines.md.

  • Canonical: docs/development/testing-guidelines.md

docs.title: CareConnect : Guide de l'utilisateur
docs.text: Dernière révision : 1 mai 2026 Version : 1.0

docs.title: Bienvenue
docs.text:
CareConnect vous aide à trouver des services sociaux à Kingston, Ontario. Ce guide vous montrera comment utiliser la plateforme.

Démarrage rapide :

  1. Allez sur le site web
  2. Tapez ce dont vous avez besoin (comme « banque alimentaire » ou « santé mentale »)
  3. Voyez une liste de services qui peuvent vous aider
  4. Appelez, envoyez un courriel ou visitez le service

docs.title: Table des matières
docs.text:
1. Pour commencer
  2. Comment rechercher
  3. Comprendre les résultats
  4. Utiliser la carte
  5. Fonctionnalités d'accessibilité
  6. Options de langue
  7. Mode hors ligne
  8. Confidentialité et vos données
  9. Questions courantes
  10. Obtenir de l'aide

docs.title: Qu'est-ce que CareConnect ?
docs.text:
CareConnect est un moteur de recherche pour les services sociaux. Il vous aide à trouver :

  • Banques alimentaires et programmes de repas
  • Soutien en cas de crise (santé mentale, prévention du suicide, violence domestique)
  • Aide au logement (refuges, aide au loyer)
  • Services de santé (cliniques, counseling, soutien à la toxicomanie)
  • Aide juridique et aide à l'immigration
  • Emploi et programmes d'éducation
  • Services familiaux (garde d'enfants, soutien parental)

Nous avons des informations sur 196 services vérifiés à Kingston.

docs.title: Qui peut utiliser ceci ?
docs.text:
Tout le monde. Ce service est gratuit et ouvert au public.

Vous n'avez pas besoin de :

  • Créer un compte
  • Donner votre nom
  • Partager des informations personnelles

docs.title: Qui a créé ceci ?
docs.text: Cette plateforme a été créée par des membres de la communauté qui voulaient faciliter la recherche d'aide à Kingston. Ce n'est pas un site web gouvernemental officiel.

docs.title: Recherche de base
docs.text:
Étape 1 : Cliquez dans la zone de recherche en haut de la page.

Étape 2 : Tapez ce dont vous avez besoin. Exemples :

  • « nourriture »
  • « santé mentale »
  • « logement »
  • « avocat gratuit »

Étape 3 : Appuyez sur Entrée ou cliquez sur le bouton de recherche.

Étape 4 : Voyez vos résultats ci-dessous.

docs.title: Conseils de recherche
docs.text:
Utilisez des mots simples :

  • ✅ Bon : « banque alimentaire »
  • ❌ Trop spécifique : « programme d'aide alimentaire d'urgence »

Essayez différents mots si vous ne trouvez pas ce dont vous avez besoin :

  • « aide emploi » OU « emploi »
  • « counseling » OU « thérapie » OU « santé mentale »

Utilisez les catégories :

  • Cliquez sur les boutons de catégorie sous la zone de recherche
  • Exemples : Nourriture, Crise, Logement, Santé

Filtrer par heures :

  • Cochez « Ouvert maintenant » pour voir uniquement les services actuellement ouverts

Rechercher près de chez vous :

  • Autorisez l'accès à la localisation pour voir les services triés par distance
